_doc: dryopea runtime parameters. The game loads this file at startup. Every entry is tunable: modders edit the value and re-launch, no rebuild required (DESIGN.md § Moddability). Each parameter has value + units + doc; the _doc fields on each section explain context. Values target a single base session of ~15-25 minutes; this is a coherent placeholder set, not a balanced one — verify in play.

world._doc: Hex grid scale + atmospheric extents. All other distances reference these.

hex_diameter.doc: Vertex-to-vertex hex size. The canonical unit; vehicle / wall / tower / range numbers all derive from this. Smaller = finer tactical resolution + more hexes to render. Larger = chunkier feel.

hex_side.doc: Edge length of one hex; = hex_diameter / 2. Derived; do not edit independently.

hex_flat_to_flat.doc: Apothem × 2 ≈ hex_diameter × √3/2. The 'short' diagonal. Used when '1 hex' means a row step rather than a diagonal step.

hex_layout.doc: Hex grid convention. Matches loft's lib/moros_* tradition for forward interop with the shared world library. The gridmesh library exposes a HexLayout adapter so 'offset_pointy_top' is also valid — switching would invalidate all neighbour-direction code.

map_play_area_default.doc: Default playable-area extents for an authored map (width × height). Smaller maps feel intimate; larger encourage scouting. Plan 04 maps specify their own.

atmosphere_haze_radius.doc: Visible radius around the player before atmospheric haze fades the world. Bounds the camera's effective view + cheap render distance. Larger = more visible area at the cost of draws; smaller = denser fog feel.

player_vehicle._doc: The player's hover unit. Smaller + brighter than enemies; noncombatant; conditionally damageable only when blocking core access.

length.doc: Along the facing axis. Sized to fit comfortably in a single hex with movement headroom.

width.doc: Across the facing axis. Two vehicles can pass abreast in a 2-hex-wide entrance.

height.doc: Reads as 'vehicle' silhouette; flatter than enemies.

hover_clearance_idle.doc: Baseline hover above local terrain. Always visibly above ground.

hover_clearance_boost.doc: Peak during boost. High enough to clear walls + steep_rock cliffs.

boost_duration.doc: Sustained boost time before auto-end. Long enough to clear a wall + recover.

boost_cooldown.doc: After boost ends, time before next boost is available.

hp_blocker.doc: Vehicle HP — relevant ONLY when the vehicle is in the blocker-damage edge case (standing between an enemy and the core). Otherwise effectively invulnerable (enemies ignore the player).

blocker_damage_rate_taken.doc: Damage rate while blocking. With multiple enemies stacking, the vehicle falls fast — intentional pressure on bad positioning.

respawn_location.doc: On destruction, the player respawns inside the core (which immediately starts the launch countdown — drive out the opening to cancel).

enemy_regular._doc: The basic robot enemy — comm-cut, drawn to the scrambler tower individually. Fits through 1-hex entrances; doesn't coordinate by default.

length.doc: Along facing — slightly larger than the player vehicle.

width.doc: Across facing — fits a 1-hex entrance with margin.

speed_engage.doc: Movement under flow-field guidance (inside scrambler bubble).

speed_approach.doc: Movement outside the bubble, along its spawn marker's direction. Same speed as engage for now.

damage_to_core_wallet.doc: When nibbling the core, this rate drains the player's point wallet (core is invulnerable; points are the proxy).

damage_to_wall.doc: When forced to attack a wall (closed perimeter, no path through), this rate. Slow — wall-breaking is a rare path, not the default.

damage_to_blocker.doc: When attacking a player/helper blocking their path to the core.

loot_value.doc: Points dropped on death; gold cube on the death hex.

pre_walk_standstill.doc: Seconds the enemy stands at its spawn marker after wave start before beginning approach. The player's scramble-decision window.

enemy_boss_phase3._doc: Heavy repair-platform enemy (phase 3, deferred). 2×2 footprint forces structural play; heals nearby regulars; never attacks towers directly.

footprint_hexes.doc: 2×2 hex bounding box. Cannot fit through 1-hex entrances; forces 2-hex gates or wall-breaking.

damage_to_wall_break.doc: Bosses are the wall-breakers. 5× a regular's rate.

repair_rate_on_regulars.doc: Healing applied to each damaged regular in range. Killing the boss stops the heal pool.

repair_range.doc: Radius from boss within which regulars are healed.

tower._doc: Pulsed-laser defensive tower. 7-hex footprint, ~6 m tall (peeks over wall_high). Decays by per-shot budget, not time.

height.doc: Body height; peeks 1 m over wall_high (5 m).

range.doc: ≈ 20 m. LOS blocked by wall_high + steep_rock; NOT by wall.

fire_interval.doc: One laser shot per second. Pulsed; the recharge gap is visible.

damage_per_shot.doc: = 3 hits to kill a regular enemy.

shot_budget_per_charge.doc: Continuous fire before the tower goes black (decayed). At 1 shot/s = 30 s of firing.

order_cost_points.doc: Debited from the wallet when the player picks up a tower beacon at the core. Failed deposit does not refund.

construction_time_helper.doc: Time for one helper to construct a tower after the beacon is deposited.

repair_time_helper_rebuild.doc: Time for a helper to repair a black tower from scratch.

boost_duration.doc: Time the top stays pink after boost; reverts to red afterwards.

boost_fire_rate_multiplier.doc: Fire interval scales by 1/this while boosted.

boost_damage_multiplier.doc: Per-shot damage scales by this while boosted.

boost_engage_time.doc: Player must hold the boost key adjacent to the tower for this long to activate.

wall._doc: Player-built barrier ground type. Two heights; both free in points, helper-time bottleneck.

wall_height.doc: Standard wall extrusion. Insects climb this easily.

wall_hp.doc: Time to break by one regular = 100 s. By a boss = 20 s.

wall_high_hp.doc: 2× wall; takes proportionally longer to break.

build_time_wall_high.doc: One helper, one wall_high hex. = 2× wall.

build_cost_points.doc: Free in points. Helper-seconds is the bottleneck.

end_ramp_slope_value.doc: Slope value applied to the open face of a wall-end hex (= hill slope). Drivable for ground units.

entrance_gap_recognition_hexes.doc: Two wall ends within 1-3 hexes (inclusive) form a recognised entrance. <1 = touching = no gap; >3 = not recognised; not the designed entrance.

helper._doc: NPC vehicle — same chassis as the player, grey, non-combat. Identity-carrying; damage = wreck + retrieve + recover, never destroyed.

roster_start.doc: Helpers emerging from the core on landing.

speed.doc: Slightly slower than the player vehicle (3 hex/s).

hp.doc: Higher than a regular enemy (30), lower than the player (100).

order_cost_points.doc: Same as tower order cost. Debited at the NPC-order face of the core.

lander_delivery_time.doc: Time between order placement and lander touchdown. Top-of-core colour interpolates over this.

recovery_time_after_retrieval.doc: Time a retrieved downed helper spends in recovery at the core before rejoining the roster.

construction_tick_hp_per_second.doc: Build rate applied at a construction site. Net = 10 s per wall, 20 s per wall_high.

carry_slot_count.doc: Same as player. Cannot carry beacon + loot simultaneously.

core._doc: Central scrambling tower / escape rocket. Invulnerable; carves the comm-jam bubble that makes defence possible.

height.doc: Taller than a max-decay tower (6 m); distinguishes core silhouette.

hp.doc: Invulnerable. Damage to the core is registered as point-wallet drain instead (see enemy_regular.damage_to_core_wallet).

scrambler_bubble_radius.doc: Radius of the comm-jam zone around the core. Inside: robots act per the wave-system rules. Outside: robots regain coordination → more dangerous. ALSO the approach→engage handoff distance (closes Q11).

launch_countdown_duration.doc: Time the player must stay inside the core's interior to launch. Bottom pulse beats faster as it elapses. Exit cancels.

launch_cancel_fade_time.doc: Time for the bottom pulse to fade after cancellation.

core_landing_area_radius.doc: Random-within zone for actual touchdown around the player's pick. Tunable: larger = more variance from pick; smaller = pick is literal.

landing_pick_edge_buffer.doc: Closest the player's pick can be to the map's playable-area boundary.

obstruction_clearance_buffer.doc: Ring around the 7-hex footprint that must be obstruction-free at landing time. The rocket auto-steers off invalid hexes searching for a valid landing.

close_spawn_disable_radius.doc: Spawn markers within this radius of the landed core are silenced for the mission. Visible to the player; just don't spawn enemies.

starter_tower_landing_offset.doc: Distance from core at which the free starter tower's lander touches down. Random within this range, random direction.

wave_system._doc: Wave shape + activation triggers. No HUD — player learns via in-world signals + memory.

wave_list.doc: Validation list — 7 waves, progressively larger. Authored content (placeholder for the eventual economy-driven model).

inter_wave_delay.doc: Wall-clock seconds between the last enemy dying and the next wave's spawn.

pre_walk_visibility_interval.doc: Seconds enemies stand visible at their spawn markers before walking. Player's scramble-decision window.

wave_1_wall_trigger.doc: One of two wave-1 triggers (whichever fires first). Number of walls the player has built. Higher = more pre-wave prep; lower = faster start.

wave_1_provocation_distance.doc: Other wave-1 trigger: a spawn marker ≥ this distance from the core, driven onto. Closer markers don't trigger; map author shapes pacing by close-vs-far placement.

economy._doc: Point-pool funded from enemy loot. Carryover via the wallet (1:1); tower-tops have no validation-tier effect yet (Q4 future model).

starting_budget_first_base.doc: Wallet at the start of a player's first mission. Lets them order 1 tower + 1 helper before earning loot.

carryover_points_ratio.doc: Fraction of the wallet at scramble that carries to the next base's starting budget. 1.0 = all unspent points carry.

tower_top_carryover_effect.doc: Validation placeholder. Future: each carried specialised top is a loadout slot for the next mission's available pool. See DESIGN.md § Q4.

camera._doc: Over-the-shoulder, locked-in-pose. Auto-reframes on sudden movement or terrain blocking the vehicle.

mode.doc: Fixed pose relative to vehicle; no player-driven rotation. The camera is automatic.

height_above_vehicle.doc: Vertical offset above the vehicle's pivot.

forward_pitch_degrees.doc: Slight pitch — player sees more ahead than behind.

swing_easing_time.doc: Auto-reframe smoothing; reads as 'the camera adjusted' rather than 'teleported'.

visible_radius_haze.doc: Mirrors world.atmosphere_haze_radius. Beyond, fade to atmospheric fog.

input._doc: Key bindings + controller mappings. Movement / position triggers do most of the work; only a handful of keys remain.

pickup_drop.doc: Single key for both pickup and deposit. Empty hands = pickup, carrying = deposit. Loot is auto-pickup on drive-over (no key).

wall_paint_toggle.doc: Toggle the wall-paint mode. Vehicle body tints red when ON; appearance of outlines as you drive confirms.

boost.doc: Hold to boost. Context-resolved: vehicle boost while moving; tower boost while adjacent to a tower.

editor_mode_toggle.doc: Editor only: flip between ground-paint and marker-place modes.

palette_select.doc: Editor only: pick the active palette entry. 11 entries match the 11 ground types.

cancel_menu.doc: Cancel a UI action / open pause menu.

mouse_or_right_stick.doc: Reserved for landing-spot pick on the planet view, editor click-to-paint, map markers. NOT camera orbit (camera is locked + auto-reframed).
